← Home

Community & Contributing

SWE-Squad is an open-source project built by and for the engineering community. Whether you are fixing bugs, building integrations, improving documentation, or sharing feedback, your contributions help make autonomous incident management better for everyone.

Why Contribute

SWE-Squad is an AI-powered platform for autonomous production incident management. Its mission: detect, triage, investigate, and resolve production incidents automatically — without human intervention for routine issues.

The platform operates through a five-stage pipeline:

Monitor →
Triage →
Investigate →
Fix →
Stability Gate

Production incidents are as diverse as the systems that generate them. Community contributions bring fresh perspectives, real-world edge cases, and domain expertise from a wide range of engineering backgrounds. Diverse viewpoints lead to more robust incident detection, more accurate triage, and more reliable automated fixes — making the platform stronger for every user.

Development Setup

Getting a local development environment up and running takes just a few steps. Make sure you have Node.js installed, then follow the commands below.

1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/swe-squad/swe-squad.git
2. Install dependencies
cd swe-squad && npm install
3. Start the development server
npm run dev
4. Build for production
npm run build
5. Run the test suite
npm run test

Areas Seeking Help

We welcome contributions across a range of areas. Whether you prefer writing code, designing systems, or crafting documentation, there is a place for you.

Additional Backends

Extend support to more LLM providers and cloud platforms so teams can use their preferred infrastructure without friction.

CI/CD Integration

Build integrations with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, and other popular CI/CD systems to embed SWE-Squad into existing delivery pipelines.

Web Dashboard

Build a real-time incident management dashboard that gives teams visibility into active incidents, agent activity, and resolution metrics.

Notification Channels

Add Slack, PagerDuty, Discord, and email notifications so the right people are alerted through the right channels at the right time.

Prompt Optimization

Improve LLM prompts for better incident analysis, more accurate root-cause identification, and higher-quality automated fixes.

Documentation

Write guides, tutorials, and API reference docs to help new users get started and help existing users go deeper.

Roadmap

The project is evolving through four phases. Here is where things currently stand.

Phase Status Focus
Phase 1 — Foundation Complete Core pipeline, multi-model routing, incident lifecycle
Phase 2 — Extensibility In Progress Plugin system, additional backends, CI/CD integration
Phase 3 — Observability Planned Web dashboard, analytics, notification channels
Phase 4 — Intelligence Planned Prompt optimization, learning from incidents, auto-tuning

Links

Get involved through these resources.

  • GitHub Repo
    https://github.com/swe-squad/swe-squad
  • GitHub Discussions

  • Contributing Guide

Code of Conduct

SWE-Squad is committed to providing a welcoming, inclusive, and harassment-free experience for everyone. We expect all contributors and community members to follow our Code of Conduct in all interactions — including issues, pull requests, discussions, and chat.
